title: inspect: provide description
body:
I'm not really sure about the content, this is what I was able to figure out from code.

Suggest edits, please.

comments.body:
The content is OK, to be a bit more precise, check what `man1/skopeo.1` says.

I am genuinely not sure about having that full documentation with examples in `--help`, though; it will be used all over the place and it kind of seems that we should present that as a common `skopeo` concept, perhaps by referring to the man page. OTOH having immediate examples in `--help` is clearly immediately useful, and we can share the text across subcommands easily. I think I’ll let @runcom make the call.

More generally, we have grown into having this transport`:`name concept all over the place, and we need to think about how to talk about that.

@runcom ?
- It needs a name we use everywhere. (Internally the API calls it an `image name`, `man1/skopeo.1` uses `image-name` mostly but calls it `-location` in the one place where we actually document the format)  I suppose the `location` was wrong, and we should just call it an `image name` everywhere. Or `skopeo name` or `namespaced container reference` or something, to avoid confusion with Docker References and URLs, at the cost of being slightly ridiculous?
- The canonical documentation should probably end up in the `skopeo.1` man page as a separate paragraph, to be referenced everywhere else.
- Separately, the output of `--help` for `skopeo` and all its subcommands needs attention (#137)
so, what shall I do with this?
@TomasTomecek sorry, been busy with other stuff, I'll try to have a look at this this week

@TomasTomecek #149 improves the `--help` output a bit, and reworks the man page to include a new “Image Names” section documenting the formats.

Do you think this is useful enough? I suppose it might still be worth it to explicitly point to the man page to explain what `IMAGE-NAME` means in each of the `--help` outputs.
#149 definitely solves my issue, will close this then; thanks!
